title: polypodiodes subamoena
intro: Polypodiodes subamoena, also known as the subamoena fern, is a type of plant belonging to the Polypodiaceae family. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Indonesia and Malaysia, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and alleviating pain, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antitumor activities. In herbal preparations, Polypodiodes subamoena is often used as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and stems are typically dried and crushed to make tea or added to other herbal concoctions.
